# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Build

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronized, reopened, closed]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Get PR title and description
        id: pr_info
        run: |
          echo "PR_TITLE=$(jq -r .pull_request.title $GITHUB_EVENT_PATH)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "PR_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r .pull_request.body $GITHUB_EVENT_PATH)" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Commit with PR title
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "GitHub Action"
          git config --global user.email "action@github.com"
          git commit --allow-empty -m "Build triggered by PR: ${{ env.PR_TITLE }}"

      - name: Send build notification
        run: |
          echo "Pull Request Title: ${{ env.PR_TITLE }}"
          echo "Pull Request Description: ${{ env.PR_DESCRIPTION }}"
          # Add your notification command here, e.g., sending to Slack or other services.
